rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical thiocyanation/cyclization of N-alkenylacrylamides with KSCN to form SCN-functionalized pyrrolinones (3a) using Mes-Acr+ photocatalyst under 455 nm light and constant current electrolysis in Bu4NOAc/HFIP.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation regenerates the excited-state-reduced photocatalyst (or oxidizes SCN-/acetate); light excites Mes-Acr+ which oxidizes thiocyanate to a thiocyanyl radical that adds to the acrylamide alkene, triggering radical cyclization.

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical after SCN addition to acrylamide alkene

electron_flow: Photoexcited Mes-Acr+* oxidizes SCN- to •SCN, which adds to the acrylamide alkene; the resulting carbon radical cyclizes onto the pendant enamide, and the cyclized radical is oxidized (anodically and/or by photocatalyst) to a cation that is deprotonated to give the pyrrolinone, while the anode reoxidizes the reduced acridine radical to close the catalytic cycle.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a fully suppressed (0% under both conditions A and B); TEMPO-SCN adduct detected by HRMS, supporting •SCN intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a fully suppressed (0% under both conditions A and B), consistent with radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: light/current on-off (control without photocatalyst)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield grows during 'on' periods and plateaus during 'off' periods for both light and current toggling, indicating both photo- and electro-input are continuously required.

probe_reagents.name: no-light / no-electricity controls
probe_reagents.observation: Without light: no product. Without electricity: only ~8% yield vs ~70% under full conditions, showing electrochemistry is essential.

notes: Two condition sets (A: with Mes-Acr+ photocatalyst; B: PC-free photoelectrosynthesis) are studied; on/off experiments in Figure S10 are for the PC-free variant (conditions B), showing that simultaneous photo- and electro-input are needed. Reaction profiles (Figure S9) confirm both light and current are required for high yield under conditions A.
